tree.setup: A campus cafe the week before the HSK exam. Your study partner, Xiao Rong, has spread three practice tests across the table and is cross-referencing them with the intensity of a conspiracy theorist connecting red strings on a corkboard. She claims she can predict which grammar points will appear on the exam based on patterns from the last five years. Her predictions have been right 80% of the time.
tree.setup_zh: HSK考试前一周的校园咖啡厅。你的学习搭档小蓉把三套模拟题铺满了桌子，像阴谋论者在软木板上连红线一样地交叉参照。她说她能根据过去五年的规律预测考试会出什么语法点。她的预测准确率是百分之八十。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 我分析了一下，今年'被'字句和'把'字句肯定会考。还有补语，每年都考。你补语掌握得怎么样？
turns.text_pinyin: wǒ fēnxī le yíxià, jīnnián 'bèi' zì jù hé 'bǎ' zì jù kěndìng huì kǎo. háiyǒu bǔyǔ, měi nián dōu kǎo. nǐ bǔyǔ zhǎngwò de zěnmeyàng?
turns.text_en: I've analyzed it -- this year 被-sentences and 把-sentences will definitely be tested. Also complements, they appear every year. How's your grasp of complements?

options.text_zh: 结果补语还行，但是程度补语我总是搞混。比如'好得很'和'很好'有什么区别？
options.text_pinyin: jiéguǒ bǔyǔ hái xíng, dànshì chéngdù bǔyǔ wǒ zǒngshì gǎo hún. bǐrú 'hǎo de hěn' hé 'hěn hǎo' yǒu shénme qūbié?
options.text_en: Result complements are OK, but I always mix up degree complements. Like what's the difference between '好得很' and '很好'?
options.feedback: Xiao Rong snaps her fingers.「好问题！'好得很'比'很好'更强调程度。而且'好得很'更口语化。」搞混 is 'mix up.' 区别 is 'difference.' Identifying your specific weak point is the first step to fixing it.

options.text_zh: 补语？我完全不会。可能来不及了吧。
options.text_pinyin: bǔyǔ? wǒ wánquán bú huì. kěnéng lái bu jí le ba.
options.text_en: Complements? I have no idea about them. It's probably too late, right?
options.feedback: Xiao Rong grabs your shoulders.「来得及！还有一周呢。我把最常考的五种补语总结了，你背下来就行。」来不及 is 'too late.' 来得及 is 'there's still time.' She won't let you give up.

options.text_zh: 还可以吧。模拟题做了几套，正确率大概百分之七十。
options.text_pinyin: hái kěyǐ ba. mónǐ tí zuò le jǐ tào, zhèngquè lǜ dàgài bǎifēn zhī qīshí.
options.text_en: Not bad I guess. I've done a few practice tests, accuracy is about 70%.
options.feedback: 「百分之七十不够。HSK三级要百分之六十过，但是你要留余地。目标应该是百分之八十以上。」正确率 is 'accuracy rate.' 留余地 means 'leave a margin.' Xiao Rong has standards.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 来，我们做一个限时练习。听力部分二十道题，给你十五分钟。计时开始！
turns.text_pinyin: lái, wǒmen zuò yí gè xiànshí liànxí. tīnglì bùfen èrshí dào tí, gěi nǐ shíwǔ fēnzhōng. jìshí kāishǐ!
turns.text_en: Come on, let's do a timed practice. Listening section, twenty questions, fifteen minutes. Timer starts now!

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: React to the timed practice after finishing.

options.text_zh: 做完了。有三道不太确定。第七题和第十二题的对话太快了，没听清关键词。
options.text_pinyin: zuò wán le. yǒu sān dào bú tài quèdìng. dì qī tí hé dì shíèr tí de duìhuà tài kuài le, méi tīng qīng guānjiàncí.
options.text_en: Done. Three I'm not sure about. Questions 7 and 12 -- the dialogues were too fast, I didn't catch the key words.
options.feedback: 「关键词没听清的话，试试先看选项再听。这样你知道要听什么。」关键词 is 'key words.' 选项 is 'answer choices.' Xiao Rong's test-taking strategy is solid.

options.text_zh: 有点紧张。时间太紧了，最后五道题是猜的。
options.text_pinyin: yǒudiǎn jǐnzhāng. shíjiān tài jǐn le, zuìhòu wǔ dào tí shì cāi de.
options.text_en: A bit nervous. Time was too tight, I guessed the last five.
options.feedback: 「考试的时候也会紧张，所以现在练是对的。猜了五道太多了，要练速度。」紧张 is 'nervous.' 猜 is 'guess.' Practice under pressure builds exam stamina.

options.text_zh: 好像没有那么难？我觉得都听懂了。
options.text_pinyin: hǎoxiàng méiyǒu nàme nán? wǒ juéde dōu tīng dǒng le.
options.text_en: Doesn't seem that hard? I think I understood everything.
options.feedback: Xiao Rong raises an eyebrow.「我们对答案看看。」She checks. You got 16 out of 20.「四道错了。你觉得都懂了但是有些理解偏了。」偏了 means 'off/deviated.' Overconfidence is its own trap.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 不错不错。对了，阅读部分你有没有什么技巧？我发现你阅读速度比听力好很多。
turns.text_pinyin: búcuò búcuò. duì le, yuèdú bùfen nǐ yǒu méiyǒu shénme jìqiǎo? wǒ fāxiàn nǐ yuèdú sùdù bǐ tīnglì hǎo hěn duō.
turns.text_en: Not bad. By the way, do you have any tips for the reading section? I noticed your reading speed is much better than your listening.

options.text_zh: 我先看问题再看文章，这样知道要找什么信息。不认识的字先跳过，看上下文猜意思。
options.text_pinyin: wǒ xiān kàn wèntí zài kàn wénzhāng, zhèyàng zhīdào yào zhǎo shénme xìnxī. bú rènshi de zì xiān tiào guo, kàn shàngxiàwén cāi yìsi.
options.text_en: I read the questions first, then the passage, so I know what info to look for. Unknown characters I skip first and guess from context.
options.feedback: Xiao Rong writes this down.「先看问题再看文章——对！这个方法省时间。上下文猜词也是HSK很重要的技能。」上下文 is 'context.' 跳过 is 'skip.' Good test strategy is a skill in itself.

options.text_zh: 没什么技巧，就是多练。我每天做两篇阅读理解。
options.text_pinyin: méi shénme jìqiǎo, jiùshì duō liàn. wǒ měi tiān zuò liǎng piān yuèdú lǐjiě.
options.text_en: No special technique, just practice a lot. I do two reading comprehension passages every day.
options.feedback: 「每天两篇？那你已经做了很多了。难怪阅读不错。」Xiao Rong looks impressed. 多练 is 'practice more.' 难怪 means 'no wonder.' Consistency beats talent.

options.text_zh: 其实阅读我也不太有信心。我们互相帮忙吧，我帮你练阅读，你帮我练听力。
options.text_pinyin: qíshí yuèdú wǒ yě bú tài yǒu xìnxīn. wǒmen hùxiāng bāngmáng ba, wǒ bāng nǐ liàn yuèdú, nǐ bāng wǒ liàn tīnglì.
options.text_en: Actually I'm not that confident in reading either. Let's help each other -- I help you practice reading, you help me practice listening.
options.feedback: 「成交！」Xiao Rong sticks out her hand for a handshake.「从明天开始，每天交换一套题。」互相 is 'mutually.' 信心 is 'confidence.' Study partnerships built on complementary strengths are the best kind.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好，今天先到这里。明天同一时间继续。记住——离考试还有七天，每天进步一点点就够了。别熬夜！
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo, jīntiān xiān dào zhèlǐ. míngtiān tóng yí shíjiān jìxù. jìzhù — lí kǎoshì hái yǒu qī tiān, měi tiān jìnbù yìdiǎndiǎn jiù gòu le. bié áo yè!
turns.text_en: OK, let's stop here for today. Same time tomorrow. Remember -- seven days until the exam, a little progress each day is enough. Don't stay up late!

tree.cultural_note: HSK (汉语水平考试, Hànyǔ Shuǐpíng Kǎoshì) is the standardized Chinese proficiency test. Common study strategies include doing 模拟题 (mónǐ tí, practice tests) and analyzing past patterns. 补语 (bǔyǔ, complements) are a major grammar focus at HSK 3-4. 别熬夜 (bié áo yè, don't pull all-nighters) is universal exam-week wisdom.
